title: Pegboard filled randomly — a tonal mosaic

rule.instruction: Tissue paper cut into 1½-inch (4 cm) squares and inserted into holes in the gray pegboard walls. All holes in the walls are filled randomly.

restrictions: #38 is a physical pegboard/tissue-paper installation, not linework — translated in SPIRIT for the pen plotter: a grid of square cells (the 'holes') covering the 300x300mm wall, with EVERY cell filled (all holes filled) by a square of hatching whose direction and spacing are chosen RANDOMLY — so spacing varies the tone, giving a random tonal/textural mosaic (the 'tissue squares'). Monochrome black on white. Applying round-4 learnings: a gentle, NATURAL hand-drawn quality (jitter ~2-4, never 'too broken'); the 16 executions span a range — grid resolution (coarse->fine), tonal contrast (even->high), and softness (crisp->hand-drawn).
